name: Tarragon Chicken Salad
recipeIngredient: 1/2 cup Dry White Wine or Vermouth
1 tbsp Chopped Fresh Tarragon or 1 tsp Dried Tarragon, crushed
1 tbsp Safflower or other Vegetable Oil
1/2 small Purple Onion, cut into thin strips
recipeInstructions:
1. Remove giblets and neck from the chicken. Rinse and pat dry.
2. Place the chicken in a large cooking bag and seal according to package directions.
3. Cut slits in the top of the bag.
4. Place the bag with the chicken in a 13 x 9 x 2 inch baking pan.
5. Bake at 350°F for 2 hours or until drumsticks move easily. Remove from the bag and let cool.
6. Skin and bone the chicken; cut into 1/2-inch pieces. Cover and chill.
7. In a small saucepan, combine wine, shallots, tarragon, salt, and pepper. Cook over medium heat until all liquid is absorbed.
8. Combine the herb mixture with yogurt in a medium bowl. Cover and chill several hours.
9. Bring 2 cups water to a boil in a medium saucepan. Add potatoes; cover, reduce heat, and simmer for 5 minutes or until tender. Remove from liquid and set aside.
10. Wash beans, trim ends, and cut into halves. Return reserved liquid to the bowl. Add beans, cover, reduce heat, and simmer for 5 minutes or until crisp-tender. Drain.
11. Combine reserved potatoes and beans with oil, salt, and pepper; toss gently. Cover and chill.
12. Spoon the chilled chicken mixture into the center of a serving platter. Arrange potatoes and green beans around the chicken mixture.
13. Approximately 280 calories per 2/3 cup chicken mixture and 1/2 cup vegetables (Fat 9.6g, Cholesterol 88mg).
